---
title: "train_bayesian"
author: "Samuel Seelan"
date: "2026-03-30"
output: html_document
---


```{r}
# Set working directory to project root (two levels up from notebooks/modeling)
project_root <- normalizePath("../..", mustWork = TRUE)
setwd(project_root)

library(brms)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(patchwork)

plot_dir <- "outputs/plots"
if (!dir.exists(plot_dir)) dir.create(plot_dir, recursive = TRUE)
```

```{r}
bayesian_data <- read.csv("data/bayesian_data.csv")
bayesian_data$date <- as.Date(bayesian_data$date)
bayesian_data$location_tier <- as.factor(bayesian_data$location_tier)
bayesian_data <- bayesian_data[order(bayesian_data$date), ]

split_date <- as.Date("2024-01-01")
train_data <- bayesian_data[bayesian_data$date < split_date, ]
test_data  <- bayesian_data[bayesian_data$date >= split_date, ]

head(bayesian_data)
n <- nrow(bayesian_data)
n

n_train <- nrow(train_data)
n_test <- nrow(test_data)
cat("Train rows:", n_train, "\n")
cat("Test rows:", n_test, "\n")
```
```{r}
plot_df_hist <- data.frame(income_needed = bayesian_data$income_needed)

p_hist <- ggplot(plot_df_hist, aes(x = income_needed)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  labs(x = "Income Needed ($)", y = "Count",
       title = "Distribution of Income Needed") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_hist)
ggsave(file.path(plot_dir, "bayesian_income_needed_hist.png"), p_hist,
       width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 200)
```

```{r}
bayes_formula <- bf(income_needed ~ MORTGAGE30US + UNRATE + CPIAUCSL +
                      zhvi_yoy_pct + inventory + days_to_pending +
                      location_tier)

get_prior(bayes_formula, data = train_data, family = gaussian())
```

```{r}
bayes_priors <- c(
  brms::prior(normal(0, 50000), class = Intercept),
  brms::prior(normal(0, 10000), class = b),
  brms::prior(exponential(1),   class = sigma)
)
```

```{r}
set.seed(42)

bayes_fit <- brm(
  formula  = bayes_formula,
  data     = train_data,
  family   = gaussian(),
  prior    = bayes_priors,
  chains   = 4,
  iter     = 2000,
  warmup   = 1000,
  cores    = 4,
  seed     = 42
)
```

```{r}
summary(bayes_fit)
plot(bayes_fit)
```

```{r}
post_samples <- as.data.frame(bayes_fit)
head(post_samples)
```

```{r}
plot_df_mort <- data.frame(coef_val = post_samples$b_MORTGAGE30US)

p_mort <- ggplot(plot_df_mort, aes(x = coef_val)) +
  geom_density(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = "Coefficient Value", y = "Density",
       title = "Posterior Distribution: MORTGAGE30US") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_mort)
ggsave(file.path(plot_dir, "bayesian_posterior_mortgage30us.png"), p_mort,
       width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 200)
```

```{r}
plot_df_zhvi <- data.frame(coef_val = post_samples$b_zhvi_yoy_pct)

p_zhvi <- ggplot(plot_df_zhvi, aes(x = coef_val)) +
  geom_density(fill = "darkorange", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = "Coefficient Value", y = "Density",
       title = "Posterior Distribution: zhvi_yoy_pct") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_zhvi)
ggsave(file.path(plot_dir, "bayesian_posterior_zhvi_yoy_pct.png"), p_zhvi,
       width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 200)
```

```{r}
post_summary <- as.data.frame(summary(bayes_fit)$fixed)
post_summary$feature <- rownames(post_summary)
post_summary <- post_summary[post_summary$feature != "Intercept", ]

plot_df_coefs <- data.frame(
  feature  = post_summary$feature,
  estimate = post_summary$Estimate,
  lower    = post_summary$`l-95% CI`,
  upper    = post_summary$`u-95% CI`
)

mort_df <- plot_df_coefs %>% filter(feature == "MORTGAGE30US")
other_df <- plot_df_coefs %>%
  filter(feature != "MORTGAGE30US") %>%
  mutate(feature = reorder(feature, estimate))

base_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

p_coef_left <- ggplot(mort_df, aes(x = estimate, y = feature)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper), height = 0.12, color = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "MORTGAGE30US",
    x = "Posterior Mean (95% Credible Interval)",
    y = "Feature"
  ) +
  base_theme

p_coef_right <- ggplot(other_df, aes(x = estimate, y = feature)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper), height = 0.12, color = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", size = 2.6) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-1000, 2000)) +
  labs(
    title = "Other Predictors",
    x = "Posterior Mean (95% Credible Interval)",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  base_theme

p_coef_split <- p_coef_left + p_coef_right +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1, 2)) +
  plot_annotation(title = "Bayesian Regression Coefficients (Split Scale)")

print(p_coef_split)
ggsave(file.path(plot_dir, "bayesian_coef_intervals.png"), p_coef_split,
       width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 200)
```

```{r}
png(file.path(plot_dir, "bayesian_pp_check.png"), width = 1200, height = 900, res = 150)
pp_check(bayes_fit, ndraws = 50)
dev.off()

pp_check(bayes_fit, ndraws = 50)

```

```{r}
preds_matrix <- posterior_predict(bayes_fit, newdata = test_data)
pred_means   <- colMeans(preds_matrix)

residuals_vec <- test_data$income_needed - pred_means
rmse <- sqrt(mean(residuals_vec^2))
mae  <- mean(abs(residuals_vec))
mape <- mean(abs(residuals_vec / test_data$income_needed)) * 100

metrics_df <- data.frame(
  split = "test",
  n_rows = nrow(test_data),
  rmse = rmse,
  mae = mae,
  mape_pct = mape
)

write.csv(metrics_df, "outputs/bayesian_metrics.csv", row.names = FALSE)

predictions_df <- data.frame(
  zip_code = test_data$zip_code,
  date = test_data$date,
  actual_income_needed = test_data$income_needed,
  predicted_income_needed = pred_means,
  residual = residuals_vec
)

write.csv(predictions_df, "outputs/bayesian_predictions.csv", row.names = FALSE)

rmse
mae
mape
metrics_df
```

```{r}
plot_df_preds <- data.frame(
  actual    = test_data$income_needed,
  predicted = pred_means
)

p_actual_pred <- ggplot(plot_df_preds, aes(x = actual, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, color = "steelblue", size = 0.8) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = "Actual Income Needed ($)", y = "Predicted Income Needed ($)",
       title = "Actual vs Predicted: Bayesian Linear Regression") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_actual_pred)
ggsave(file.path(plot_dir, "bayesian_actual_vs_predicted.png"), p_actual_pred,
       width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 200)
```

```{r}
saveRDS(bayes_fit, "outputs/bayesian_model.rds")
```
